zh.lifeSummary: 當David Hamilton於1334年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡焦福時，他的父親Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow24歲，母親Margaret Leslie19歲。他於1369年與Jonetta Keith在蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1392年5月14日在他的家鄉享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当David Hamilton于1334年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾卡焦福时，他的父亲Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow24岁，母亲Margaret Leslie19岁。他于1369年与Jonetta Keith在苏格兰艾斯爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1392年5月14日在他的家乡享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hamilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from what is now a deserted village in the parish of Barkby, Leicestershire. This is named from Old English hamel ‘crooked’ + dūn ‘hill’. Hamilton near Glasgow was founded by the English Hamiltons and named after them, and later bearers took their surname from the Scottish place. In the north of Ireland, where many Hamiltons settled in the 17th century, this surname may sometimes have been adopted for Hamill . It has also been adopted as an Anglicized form of southern Irish Ó hUrmholtaigh.

History: This name is borne by one of the most distinguished families of the Scottish nobility; they hold many titles, including marquessate and dukedom of Hamilton, the marquessate of Douglas, the dukedom of Abercorn, and the earldom of Haddington. They are descended from Walter FitzGilbert de Hameldone, a Norman baron who gave his support to Robert the Bruce in the 13th century. A member of this family was Sir William Hamilton (1730–1803), a British diplomat and archaeologist, whose wife, Lady Emma Hamilton (c. 1765–1815), became the mistress of Admiral Horatio Nelson. A branch of the family was established in Ireland by Sir Frederick Hamilton (died 1646), who served in the Swedish army of Gustavus Adolphus. He later became governor of Ulster, and his descendants were created viscounts Boyne. The family have given their name to Newtownhamilton and Hamiltonsbaron in County Armagh. Another branch of the family were to be found in Denmark, where Henrik Albertsen Hamilton (1588–1648) was a noted Latin poet. A 17th-century example of a Hamilton from Glasgow, Scotland, is recorded in the Netherlands, where the name is found in the form Hamelton. Another Scottish Hamilton, James, went to the West Indies in the 18th century and was the father of Alexander Hamilton (1755–1804), who arrived in NY in 1772 and became the first US secretary of the Treasury.

variants.name: Hamill
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Haineville or Henneville in Manche, France, named from the ancient Germanic personal name Hagano + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
English (Lancashire): nickname for a maimed person or someone with a distinctive scar, from Middle English, Old English hamel ‘mutilated, scarred’.
Irish (Ulster): according to MacLysaght, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁdhmaill ‘descendant of Ádhmall’, which he derives from ádhmall ‘active’.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When David Hamilton was born in 1334, in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow, was 24 and his mother, Margaret Leslie, was 19. He married Jonetta Keith in 1369, in Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 14 May 1392, in his hometown, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque David Hamilton est né en 1334, à Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Leslie, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Jonetta Keith en 1369, à Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 14 mai 1392, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als David Hamilton 1334 in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow 24 und seine Mutter Margaret Leslie 19. Er hat 1369 in Ayrshire, Scotland Jonetta Keith geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Mai 1392 im Alter von 58 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando David Hamilton è nato il nel 1334 nel Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Leslie, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Jonetta Keith nel 1369, nel Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 14 maggio 1392, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: David Hamilton(이)가 1334년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Leslie(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1369년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 Jonetta Keith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1392년 5월 14일 그의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Jonetta Keith в 1369 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 14 мая 1392 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 58 лет.
